title: TCP Congestion Control
description: Algorithms that prevent TCP senders from overwhelming the network by dynamically adjusting transmission rate.
fullExplanation: TCP congestion control prevents network collapse by limiting how fast senders inject data. The sender maintains a congestion window (cwnd) — the maximum bytes in flight. cwnd starts small and grows until congestion is detected (packet loss or ECN). Classic algorithms (Reno, CUBIC) treat packet loss as a congestion signal. Modern algorithms (BBR) model bandwidth and RTT directly. The effective send rate = min(cwnd, rwnd) / RTT.

phases.description: cwnd starts at 1-10 MSS. Doubles every RTT (exponential growth) until ssthresh reached.
cwnd grows by 1 MSS per RTT (linear growth) after ssthresh. Probes for more bandwidth carefully.
3 duplicate ACKs = packet loss detected. Retransmit immediately without waiting for timeout.
After fast retransmit: ssthresh = cwnd/2, cwnd = ssthresh. Resume congestion avoidance.

algorithms.name: TCP Tahoe
algorithms.description: Original. On loss: ssthresh=cwnd/2, cwnd=1, restart slow start.

algorithms.name: TCP Reno
algorithms.description: Adds fast recovery. On 3 dup ACKs: ssthresh=cwnd/2, cwnd=ssthresh (not 1).

algorithms.name: TCP CUBIC
algorithms.description: Default in Linux. Uses cubic function for cwnd growth — better for high-BDP networks.

algorithms.name: TCP BBR
algorithms.description: Google's model-based algorithm. Measures bottleneck bandwidth and RTT directly. Ignores loss as signal.

algorithms.name: QUIC/BBR
algorithms.description: BBR running over QUIC/UDP — used by Google, YouTube, Chrome.

keyFacts: cwnd (congestion window): sender-side limit on bytes in flight
rwnd (receive window): receiver-side limit — flow control
ssthresh (slow start threshold): boundary between slow start and congestion avoidance
CUBIC: default in Linux since kernel 2.6.19. Optimized for high-bandwidth, high-latency links.
BBR: doesn't treat packet loss as congestion signal — better on lossy links (WiFi, cellular)
ECN (Explicit Congestion Notification): routers signal congestion without dropping packets
